title: Annotation framework: types and compiler
analysis: Task 264 (dependency) added parsed Annotation { name: String, args: Vec<Expr>, span: SourceSpan } to reify-syntax and the annotations: Vec<Annotation> field to all 10 declaration AST types (StructureDef, OccurrenceDef, FnDef, TraitDecl, FieldDef, PurposeDef, EnumDecl, ImportDecl, ConstraintDef, UnitDecl). Task 265 must: (1) define a compiled Annotation type in reify-types with AnnotationArg enum for resolved argument values, (2) add annotations fields to all 5 compiled output types (TopologyTemplate, CompiledFunction, CompiledTrait, CompiledField, CompiledPurpose), (3) implement compiler lowering from parsed syntax::Annotation to compiled types::Annotation by converting Expr args to AnnotationArg values, (4) validate known annotations against valid contexts. Adding the annotations field to CompiledFunction (in reify-types) and TopologyTemplate/CompiledTrait/etc. (in reify-compiler) requires mechanical updates to all struct construction sites across reify-compiler, reify-test-support, reify-expr, and reify-compiler tests. The trait_conformance_tests.rs file alone has ~30 direct CompiledTrait {} constructions and reify-expr has ~10 direct CompiledFunction {} constructions that all need annotations: vec![].

prerequisites.description: Create crates/reify-types/src/annotation.rs with Annotation struct and AnnotationArg enum. Annotation has fields: name (String), args (Vec<AnnotationArg>), span (SourceSpan). AnnotationArg is an enum with variants: String(String), Int(i64), Real(f64), Bool(bool), Ident(String). Derive Debug, Clone on Annotation; derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq on AnnotationArg.
Add `pub mod annotation;` to crates/reify-types/src/lib.rs and add re-exports: `pub use annotation::{Annotation, AnnotationArg};`

Add `pub annotations: Vec<crate::annotation::Annotation>` field to CompiledFunction in crates/reify-types/src/expr.rs (after content_hash). Then fix ALL downstream CompiledFunction {} construction sites by adding `annotations: vec![]`: (a) crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs line ~5293, (b) crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs (~6 sites in test helpers make_double_fn, make_fn_with_let, make_factorial_fn, make_infinite_fn, area_fn in eval_user_fn_dimension_args, process1+process2 in eval_user_fn_overload_by_arity), (c) crates/reify-expr/tests/collection_eval_tests.rs (~4 sites: double_fn, is_even_fn, add_fn, is_even_fn), (d) crates/reify-expr/tests/lambda_eval_tests.rs (~2 sites: double_fn instances).

Add `pub annotations: Vec<reify_types::Annotation>` field to TopologyTemplate, CompiledTrait, CompiledField, CompiledPurpose in crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs. Then fix ALL construction sites in this file: (a) TopologyTemplate {} in compile_entity (~line 3850): add annotations: vec![], (b) CompiledTrait {} in compile_trait (~line 1999) and check_trait_conformance_chain flat_trait (~line 267): add annotations: vec![], (c) CompiledField {} in compile_field (~line 5390): add annotations: vec![], (d) CompiledPurpose {} in compile_purpose (~line 2189): add annotations: vec![].

Fix CompiledTrait {} construction sites in crates/reify-compiler/tests/trait_conformance_tests.rs (~30 instances including empty_trait() helper, individual test functions, make_sub_trait() helper, make_port_trait-style helpers). Add `annotations: vec![]` to every CompiledTrait struct literal.

Fix construction sites in crates/reify-test-support/src/builders.rs: (a) TopologyTemplateBuilder field + build() (~line 678), (b) Two CompiledTraitBuilder::build() methods (~lines 1138 and 1660), (c) CompiledField {} constructions in field builders, (d) CompiledPurpose {} constructions in purpose builders. Add `annotations: vec![]` to each struct literal. Also add an `annotations(Vec<Annotation>)` builder method to TopologyTemplateBuilder for setting annotations in test fixtures.

steps.description: Write test in crates/reify-types/tests/exports.rs: new test fn `annotation_types_exported` that constructs Annotation { name: "test".into(), args: vec![AnnotationArg::String("msg".into())], span: SourceSpan::new(0,0) } and exercises all AnnotationArg variants (String, Int, Real, Bool, Ident). Verifies types are accessible from crate root via `reify_types::Annotation` and `reify_types::AnnotationArg`.

Verify prerequisites pre-1 through pre-6 make the project compile. Run `cargo check -p reify-types -p reify-compiler -p reify-expr -p reify-test-support` and fix any compilation errors. The exports test from step-1 should pass.

Write failing test in crates/reify-compiler/tests/annotation_compile_tests.rs: `annotation_on_structure_propagates`. Parse `@test structure S { param x : Real }` via compile_module helper (parse + compile pattern from other test files). Assert module has no error-level diagnostics, templates[0].annotations.len() == 1, annotations[0].name == "test", annotations[0].args.is_empty().

Implement annotation lowering for structures/occurrences: (1) Add `annotations: &'a [reify_syntax::Annotation]` field to EntityDefRef struct (~line 2850), update From<StructureDef> and From<OccurrenceDef> impls to pass &s.annotations / &o.annotations. (2) Write standalone fn `lower_annotations(parsed: &[reify_syntax::Annotation], diagnostics: &mut Vec<Diagnostic>) -> Vec<reify_types::Annotation>` that maps each parsed annotation: name passes through, args are lowered from Expr to AnnotationArg (NumberLiteral with value==floor and fits i64→Int else→Real, StringLiteral→String, BoolLiteral→Bool, Ident→Ident, other→diagnostic warning and skip arg). (3) In compile_entity, call lower_annotations(structure.annotations, diagnostics) and set TopologyTemplate.annotations field.

Write test: `annotation_with_args_on_function_propagates`. Parse `@deprecated("use new_calc") fn old_calc(x: Real) -> Real { x }`. Assert functions[0].annotations[0].name == "deprecated", annotations[0].args.len() == 1, annotations[0].args[0] == AnnotationArg::String("use new_calc".into()).
In compile_function(), add annotation lowering: call lower_annotations(&fn_def.annotations, diagnostics) and set annotations field in the CompiledFunction return struct literal (~line 5293).

Write three tests: (a) `annotation_on_trait_propagates`: parse `@deprecated trait Measurable { param width : Length }`, assert trait_defs[0].annotations[0].name == "deprecated". (b) `annotation_on_field_propagates`: parse `@deprecated field temp_field : Point3 -> Real = analytical { 0.0 }`, assert fields[0].annotations[0].name == "deprecated". (c) `annotation_on_purpose_propagates`: parse a structure S + `@deprecated purpose P(subject: S) { minimize subject.x }`, assert compiled_purposes[0].annotations[0].name == "deprecated".

Add annotation lowering in compile_trait (call lower_annotations(&trait_decl.annotations, diagnostics), set CompiledTrait.annotations), compile_field (call lower_annotations(&field_def.annotations, diagnostics), set CompiledField.annotations), and compile_purpose (call lower_annotations(&purpose_def.annotations, diagnostics), set CompiledPurpose.annotations).

Write validation tests: (a) `known_annotation_valid_context_no_warning`: `@test structure S { param x : Real }` produces no warning-level diagnostics about annotations. (b) `known_annotation_invalid_context_produces_warning`: `@test field f : Point3 -> Real = analytical { 0.0 }` produces a warning diagnostic containing '@test' and 'field'. (c) `deprecated_valid_on_any_context`: `@deprecated fn f(x: Real) -> Real { x }` and `@deprecated structure S { param x : Real }` produce no annotation warnings. (d) `unknown_annotation_produces_warning`: `@foobar structure S { param x : Real }` produces a warning diagnostic containing 'foobar' and 'unknown'.

Add annotation context validation. Define fn `validate_annotations(annotations: &[reify_types::Annotation], context: &str, span: SourceSpan, diagnostics: &mut Vec<Diagnostic>)` where context is one of "structure", "occurrence", "function", "trait", "field", "purpose". Known annotation rules: @test valid on [structure, occurrence, function]; @optimized valid on [structure, occurrence]; @solver_hint valid on [structure, occurrence]; @deprecated valid on any context. Misplaced known annotations emit Diagnostic::warning. Unknown annotation names emit Diagnostic::warning with 'unknown annotation' message. Call validate_annotations at each lowering site (compile_entity, compile_function, compile_trait, compile_field, compile_purpose) after lower_annotations.

Write comprehensive edge-case tests: (a) `multiple_annotations_all_preserved`: `@test @deprecated("old") structure S { param x : Real }` has 2 annotations, first.name=="test" and second has String arg. (b) `annotation_arg_types_lowered`: `@config("name", 42, 3.14, true, mechanical) structure S { param x : Real }` has 5 args: String, Int(42), Real(3.14), Bool(true), Ident("mechanical"). (c) `annotation_on_occurrence_propagates`: `@test occurrence Heat { param temp : Real }` propagates to template with entity_kind == Occurrence.

Verify all tests pass. Fix any issues with annotation argument lowering (ensure NumberLiteral distinguishes Int from Real: if value == value.floor() && value.abs() < i64::MAX as f64, use AnnotationArg::Int(value as i64), else AnnotationArg::Real(value)). Ensure occurrence annotations flow through EntityDefRef correctly. Run full test suite: `cargo test -p reify-types -p reify-compiler -p reify-expr -p reify-test-support`.

design_decisions.decision: AnnotationArg is a simple enum of literal values (String, Int, Real, Bool, Ident) rather than reusing CompiledExpr
design_decisions.rationale: Annotations are compile-time metadata, not runtime expressions. Using a simple value enum keeps the API clean, prevents misuse, and avoids pulling in the full expression tree for what should be constant data. Complex expressions in annotation positions emit a diagnostic and are skipped.

Downstream consumers (LSP hover, deprecation warnings, test discovery) need to point back to the annotation source location. While validation happens in the compiler, the span enables richer downstream tooling.

design_decisions.decision: Validation uses warnings (not errors) for context violations and unknown annotations
design_decisions.rationale: Forward compatibility: new annotations may be added in the future, and third-party annotations may be valid. Warnings inform the user without blocking compilation. This matches the pragmas pattern already in use.

design_decisions.decision: Annotation lowering is a standalone function reused across all compile_X functions rather than per-type logic
design_decisions.rationale: The lowering logic (Expr to AnnotationArg conversion) is identical regardless of declaration type. A single lower_annotations() function eliminates duplication and ensures consistent behavior.

design_decisions.decision: NumberLiteral args are classified as Int when value == floor(value) and fits in i64, else Real
design_decisions.rationale: The parser produces f64 for all numbers. Integer annotation args (e.g., @solver_hint(max_iterations, 100)) are common and downstream consumers will want to match on Int without lossy conversion.

design_decisions.decision: annotations: Vec<Annotation> field added to CompiledFunction in reify-types rather than a wrapper in reify-compiler
design_decisions.rationale: CompiledFunction is the canonical compiled function type shared across all crates. Adding annotations here keeps the single-source-of-truth principle. The cost is mechanical updates to ~15 construction sites across reify-expr and reify-compiler, but these are trivial (annotations: vec![]).

reuse.how: Use for all annotation validation diagnostics: wrong context warnings and unknown annotation warnings
New annotation_compile_tests.rs follows the same pattern: parse source string, call reify_compiler::compile(), assert on compiled output
Annotation hashes should be included in parent type content_hash computation at each compile_X function for incremental build correctness
Extend EntityDefRef with annotations: &'a [reify_syntax::Annotation] field, update From impls to pass through from StructureDef/OccurrenceDef
Add .annotations() builder method so test fixtures can set annotations on templates
Use to filter diagnostics in annotation validation tests, checking for warning-level diagnostics on invalid contexts
